feedback.text: It would be great if this could extend to not just hardware, but software as well, including not only games (as many have mentioned already), but software in general.. If you buy a piece of software, wether that be physically, or via a digital license that gives the initial impression that you will own the software you buy instead of essentially "renting" it, and it is made so it needs a server to operate, there should be a fallback in place for it to remain functional when that server goes offline, maybe in a limped state if need be, but functional nonetheless. So it would be nice if this digital fairness act could extend to software in general, which should include games, such as outlined in the "Stop Killing Games" initiative.
